title: EDHOC responder does not validate METHOD_CORR in Message 1
description:
File: rust/lichen-oscore/src/edhoc.rs
Lines: 677

The METHOD_CORR byte is parsed but not validated:
```rust
let _method_corr = msg1[0];
```

The code assumes method 0 (SIGN_SIGN) with correlation 1. Per RFC 9528:
- method_corr = method * 4 + corr
- method 0 = Signature (Ed25519 for Suite 0)
- method 1/2/3 = Static DH variants (not implemented)

If an initiator sends method 1-3 (Static DH), the responder will:
1. Accept the message
2. Fail during signature verification (since the initiator sends MAC not signature)
3. Return SignatureVerification error instead of UnsupportedMethod

Impact: Confusing error messages; minor protocol compliance issue.

Fix: Validate method_corr == 1 (method 0, corr 1) and return appropriate error for unsupported methods.
close_reason: Added validation for METHOD_CORR in EdhocResponder::process_message_1; returns UnsupportedMethod for unsupported methods. Updated error enum and Display impl. Merge resolved and committed.
